title: In situ expandable tubulars
abstract: Expandable tube members that are fabricated from a composite material that includes a structural plastic, which structural plastic includes phase change materials that undergo a permanent expansion upon exposure to wellbore conditions. This permanent expansion of the structural plastic causes the expandable tube member to expand radially and/or longitudinally without the use of an expansion tool. The expandable tube member can be used to control fluid loss, patch wells, stabilize a formation in a wellbore, enhance flow, provide sand screening, and repair damaged pipes, casings, or liners.
claims:
Claims What is claimed: 1) An expandable tube member formed of a body having an outer surface and an internal cavity that extends a longitudinal length of said tube member, said body at least partially formed of an expandable composite material, said expandable composite material includes a polymer matrix and a plurality of expandable filler particles in said polymer matrix, said composite material prior to being expanded having a strength exceeding 5000 psig and stiffness exceeding 200,000 psig, said expandable composite material is formulated to expand when said expandable filler particles are partially or fully activated by one or more activation mechanisms selected from the group consisting of a) change in temperature about said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler from the surface of a ground cavity to a particular location in said ground cavity, b) exposure of said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler to a certain temperature or temperature range, c) change in pH about said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler, d) exposure of said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler to a certain pH or pH range, e) change in salinity about said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler, f) exposure of said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler to a certain salinity or salinity range, g) exposure of said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler to an activation element or compound, h) change in pressure about said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler from the surface of a ground cavity to a particular location in said ground cavity, i) exposure of said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler to a certain pressure or pressure range, j) electrical stimulation of said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler, k) exposure of said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler to a certain sound frequency, and 1) exposure of said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler to a certain electromagnetic frequency. 2) The expandable tube member as defined in claim 1, wherein said expandable composite material is formulated to expand when said expandable filler particles are partially or fully activated by one or more activation mechanisms selected from the group consisting of a) change in temperature about said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler from the surface of a ground cavity to a particular location in said ground cavity, b) exposure of said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler to a certain temperature or temperature range, c) change in pH about said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler, d) exposure of said 

 expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler to a certain pH or pH range, e) change in salinity about said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler, f) exposure of said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler to a certain salinity or salinity range, g) exposure of said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler to an activation element or compound, said activation element or compound including one or more components selected from the group consisting of water and CO2, h) change in pressure about said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler from the surface of a ground cavity to a particular location in said ground cavity, and i) exposure of said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler to a certain pressure or pressure range. 3) The expandable tube member as defined in claim 1, wherein said expandable composite material is partially or fully formed of a thermoplastic or thermosetting polymer matrix and a plurality of expandable filler particles that are reactive to one or more components selected from the group consisting of water and C0 2 . 4) The expandable tube member as defined in claim 2, wherein said expandable composite material is partially or fully formed of a thermoplastic or thermosetting polymer matrix and a plurality of expandable filler particles that are reactive to one or more components selected from the group consisting of water and C0 2 . 5) The expandable tube member as defined in claim 1 , wherein said expandable filler includes one or more materials selected from the group consisting of magnesium oxide, calcium oxide, lithium oxide, borate, selenium, iron, magnesium, manganese, germanium, calcium, aluminum, sodium, lithium, zinc, other oxidizable metal with a high Biddeford ratio, water- or C0 2 -swellable clay, water- or C0 2 -swellable phosphate, water- or C0 2 -swellable carbide, water- or C0 2 - swellable silicate, water- or C0 2 -swellable borate, and water- or C0 2 -swellable sulfate material. 6) The expandable tube member as defined in any one of claims 2 to 4, wherein said expandable filler includes one or more materials selected from the group consisting of magnesium oxide, calcium oxide, lithium oxide, borate, selenium, iron, magnesium, manganese, germanium, calcium, aluminum, sodium, lithium, zinc, other oxidizable metal with a high Biddeford ratio, 

 water- or C0 2 -swellable clay, water- or CO?-swellable phosphate, water- or C0 2 -swellable carbide, water- or C0 2 -swellable silicate, water- or C0 2 -swellable borate, and water- or CO 2 - swellable sulfate material. 7) The expandable tube member as defined in claim 1, wherein said polymer matrix includes a thermoplastic or thermoset having a plastic strain to failure of more than 10% and a strength above 5,000 psig, said polymer matrix including one or more compounds selected from the group consisting of nylon/polyarylates, polycarbonates, polysulfones, polyureas, polyesters, epoxys, polypropylene, polyethylene, and other engineering plastics. 8) The expandable tube member as defined in any one of claims 2 to 6, wherein said polymer matrix includes a thermoplastic or nylon/polyarylates, polycarbonates, polysulfones, polyureas, polyesters, epoxys, polypropylene, polyethylene, and other engineering plastics. 9) The expandable tube member as defined in claim 1, wherein said expandable tube member is in the form of a 1) liner, 2) screen, 3) pipe section, 4) plug, 5) patch, 6) packer, 7) device to control lost circulation or fluid inflow during drilling operations, 8) device to repair or seal a leak, 9) device to enhance fluid flow, 10) device to provide sand screening, 1 1) device to repair damaged pipes, casings, or liners, and/or 12) device to structure, said reinforcement structure includes one or more materials selected from the group 

 consisting of reinforcing fibers, flakes, ribbons, ribs, mesh, non-expandable filler, and other internal structures. 12) The expandable tube member as defined in any one or claims 2 to 10, further including a reinforcement structure, said reinforcement structure includes one or more materials selected from the group consisting of reinforcing fibers, flakes, ribbons, ribs, mesh, non-expandable filler, and other internal structures. 13) The expandable tube member as defined in claim 11, wherein said reinforcement structure is configured to perform at least one or more functions selected from expansion of said expandable tube member, 4) control an amount of liner expansion of said expandable tube member, and 5) increase burst and/or buckling limits of said expandable tube member. 14) The further including an outer coating that at least partially covers said outer surface of said body, said outer coating selected from the group of materials consisting of an expandable material and a non-expandable material, said outer coating configured to perform at least one or more functions selected from the group consisting of 1) partially or fully conform to an exterior surface of a well bore wall or pipe during or after expansion of said expandable tube member, 2) reduce friction as said expandable tube member is inserted into a pipe or wellbore prior to expansion or prior to full expansion of said expandable tube member, 3) form a partial or full liquid seal between said outer coating and the inner wall surface of the wellbore or a pipe during or after expansion of said expandable tube member, 4) 

 facilitate in centering said expandable tube member in the wellbore or a pipe during or after expansion of said expandable tube member, 5) secure said expandable tube member in position relative to the inner wall surface of the wellbore or a pipe during or after expansion of said expandable tube member, 6) limit or prevent a flow of fluid into fractures in a wall cavity of a well bore or damaged degradable material, said outer coating configured to delay or prevent activation of said expandable filler particles until said outer coating becomes partially or fully degraded.

range, j) electrical stimulation of said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler, k) exposure of said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler to a certain sound frequency, and 1) exposure of said expandable tube member and/or said expandable filler to a certain electromagnetic frequency; b. inserting said expandable tube member into a desired location in a wellbore, a pipe or a tube prior to said expandable tube member being fully expanded; c. activating said expandable filler particles in said expandable tube member prior to or after said expandable tube member is positioned in said desired location in a wellbore, a pipe or a tube; and, d. maintaining said expandable tube member in said desired location in a wellbore, a pipe or a tube until said expandable tube member has sufficient expanded to perform its desired use. 

 26) The method as defined in claim 25, wherein said desired use of said expandable tube member when in a partially or fully expanded stated includes one or more uses selected from the group consisting of 1) controlling lost circulation or fluid inflow during drilling operations, 2) repairing or sealing a leak, 3) stabilizing a wellbore, 4) used as part of a swellable packer, and 5) deployed on a drillstring without tripping the drillstring.
